seo.og_title: Kinde — Pricing, Developer Experience & Best Use Cases
seo.og_description: Kinde for Next.js App Router: Easy learning curve, Free – $300/mo, 15 minutes setup. Teams wanting feature flags + auth in one platform, generous free tier for early growth

hero.headline: Kinde: Complete Review for Next.js Developers
hero.subheadline: A thorough breakdown of Kinde's pricing, developer experience, and fit for Next.js App Router projects.

intro_paragraphs: For Next.js developers evaluating auth solutions, Kinde stands out for teams wanting feature flags + auth in one platform, generous free tier for early growth. With a rating of 4.3★ and a Next.js integration score of 7/10, it is one of the most approachable options available.
who_should_use_paragraphs: Kinde works best for Next.js projects where ease of integration matters most. The easy learning curve means your whole team can be productive within hours. We recommend it for teams that want a managed solution with minimal ops overhead.
pricing_context: Kinde's pricing appears competitive at $300/mo for Scale plan, but the 7,500 MAU limit on the free tier is tight for a growing app. At 15K MAU you hit $300/mo, and advanced features like MFA and SSO require the Growth plan at $600/mo. For a Next.js startup with modest revenue, auth costs of $300-600/mo are hard to justify. Clerk's free tier (10K MAU) or Supabase Auth (50K MAU free) offer better economics at scale.

faq.question: Is Kinde good for Next.js App Router?
faq.answer: Kinde has a Next.js integration score of 7/10. Kinde's Next. Kinde is a capable option for App Router projects.

faq.question: How long does it take to set up Kinde with Next.js?
faq.answer: Setup time is approximately 15 minutes with an easy learning curve. js integration centers around the @kinde-oss/kinde-auth-nextjs package.
